root.narration: You are about to hit the ball, but first you need to hold your racket. Your coach asks you to show your grip. How do you hold it?

choices.text: Shake hands with the racket handle like you are greeting a friend, then wrap your fingers around it
Grab the racket tightly in the middle of the handle with your whole fist
Hold the racket up near the head where the strings are

outcome.headline: Perfect handshake grip!
outcome.explanation: The handshake grip is how most beginners learn to hold a tennis racket. It gives you good control and lets you hit the ball straight over the net.
outcome.whatToRemember: Think of shaking hands with your racket. Your hand should be relaxed but firm on the handle, not too tight.

keyTerms.definition: A basic tennis grip where you hold the racket like you are shaking hands with it, great for hitting forehands

outcome.headline: Close, but loosen up!
outcome.explanation: Grabbing the handle is the right area, but squeezing too hard makes your arm stiff. You will not be able to swing smoothly if your grip is too tight.
outcome.whatToRemember: Hold the racket firmly but not too tight. Imagine holding a bird -- tight enough it will not fly away, but gentle enough you will not hurt it.

outcome.headline: Wrong spot on the racket!
outcome.explanation: Holding the racket near the strings means you have no control over your swing. The handle is where your hand belongs so you can guide the racket properly.
outcome.whatToRemember: Always hold the racket by the handle at the bottom. That is where your hand has the most control.
